text: There is no social restriction here. There is only restriction not to sit both men and women in the same training venue.
Yes, there are social norms which regulate the various works and gender responsibilities in the agricultural activities.
Young men can do all sort of works in crop cultivation. On the other hand, women have to start some household works

cleaning house premises, wash all utensils, clean the cowshed and cook foods and serve to all family members.  These are all done by women. No men or boys not familiar or habituate to do these works. These are treated as feminine works.
collecting fodder for cows, preparing lands, transplanting, weeding, sowing, harvesting etc. is men’s work. And carrying loads from field, selling agricultural products in market are also men’s work.
Caring and raising chickens, duck and cultivating vegetables in the kitchen garden are task of woman.
For social reasons, women cannot participate in practical agricultural activities. They are not allowed to carry loads from the field
keyword: women cannot participate; not allowed to carry

text: But women practically work and assist husband in the crop fields nowadays but it was not possible before.
In our village, no women are allowed to harvest wheat and rice. This is men’s job and their domain where women cannot go and participate.
keyword: This is men’s job; women cannot go and participate.

text: Similarly women will be highly discouraged to sell wheat or rice in the local market.
Only those women can move to this domain if she is widow, no father, brother or grown-up son. But for a young woman there will be problem and social restriction
Women work in the field is not a regular practice in our village
there is also problem for young women participating with men in a training session or other activities.
Social restrictions are for women not for men. So, men can grow and sell any products in nearby or distant market there is no prohibition for his activities.
Land may be entitled by both husband and wife but all lands are supervised by husband

text: older son may take decision consulting with mother if father died or absent

In all cultivable lands of a household, male members work.
women work to their husbands’ lands but cannot make major decision.

Woman shall talk to her grown-up son and her husband if he is still alive. And if her husband died then she will consults with her in-laws
